title: Barnette's Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
context_text: Let $\mathcal{B}$ be the class of all cubic, 3-connected, planar, bipartite graphs. Barnette's Conjecture from 1969 is the central subject of this paper. Since all known counterexamples to Tait's conjecture are non-bipartite and all known counterexamples to Tutte's conjecture are non-planar, Barnette's conjecture represents a natural compromise, and has received considerable attention with partial solutions known for some subclasses of $\mathcal{B}$.
paper_contribution: Provides a matching-theoretic translation showing that Conjecture 1.1 holds if and only if all cubic, 3-connected, Pfaffian, bipartite graphs are Hamiltonian (Theorem 1.3), and reduces the conjecture to cubic, planar braces (Lemma 1.2), also proving analogous reductions for four stronger Hamiltonicity properties in cubic, 3-connected, bipartite graphs without requiring planarity.
